question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 1–5

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

Poppy Reserve

Almond Valley Poppy Reserve is a nature reserve
• The poppies will be at their best at the end of the 1 ………….. .
• The poppies on the hills are 2 …………. in colour.
The reserve is located to the 3 ……………. of Lakeside.
• The best time for photography is mid-afternoon.
• Wear 4 ……….. because of rattlesnakes.
• The reserve is a 5 …………. park.

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 11–15

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Re-creating life in an Iron Age village

11 Jim says that in the Iron Age village

A he is sometimes cold at night.

B he always gets up early.

C he has time to himself.

12 What does Jim say about the food they eat in the village?

A It takes time to prepare.

B It is difficult to get used to.

C It has to be eaten quickly.

13 Jim says that at the evening meal

A they are sometimes too tired to eat.

B they mainly have fruit and berries.

C they talk about what they've done.

14 The most important job for the blacksmiths is

A to mend broken tools and implements.

B to maintain a good source of heat.

C to collect sufficient firewood.

15 Jim likes the fact that in the village, people

A do varied activities.

B share their knowledge.

C get a lot of exercise.

options.label: A
options.text: he is sometimes cold at night.

options.label: B
options.text: he always gets up early.

options.label: C
options.text: he has time to himself.

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–I.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 16–20

Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose FIVE answers from the box and write the correct letter, A–I.

MAKING AN IRON AGE ROUNDHOUSE

Mark centre of planned house with a 16 …………

↓

Mark out circle for outer boundary

↓

Dig ground using tools made from 17 ………..

↓

Insert wall posts (Iron Age builders preferred wood from oak trees)

↓

Slot more stakes into upright posts

↓

Insert centre post and build a wooden 18 ………… around it

↓

Add main supporting beams and ring beams

↓

Complete roof framework using 19 ………… wood rods

↓

Cover roof with reed bundles of 20 ………… lengths

↓

Daub with mixture of clay, dung and straw

A bone

B different

C equal

D elm

E hazel

F iron

G peg

H roof

I scaffold

question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 21–24

Choose the correct answer.

Presentation on how jobs might change in the future

21 What do the students find surprising about the way that complaints were dealt with at the call centre?

A the way that customers responded to it

B the employees’s lack of enthusiasm for it

C the kind of questions employees were obliged to ask

22 What does Emma say about the report by Jerry Kaplan?

A It shows how machines will respond to human needs.

B It emphasises the variety of roles that machines will have.

C It explains the limitations of machines.

23 What is Nick's reaction to the article The Human Cloud?

A He agrees that companies should focus on making savings.

B He supports the concept of greater flexibility for employees.

C He approves of businesses spending more on recruiting experts.

24 What do the students agree about the use of technology to collect information about employees?

A Cameras are an effective means of maintaining security.

B Sometimes productivity is more important than individual privacy.

C Employees may object to certain types of monitoring.

options.label: C
options.text: the kind of questions employees were obliged to ask

question_groups.prompt_html:

B whether it should be removed completely
C whether it is clear enough

H which of them will present it

question_groups.instruction:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
question_groups.prompt_html:
Questions 31–40

Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.

HEALTH ON THE NIGHT SHIFT

Background:
• in number of night workers because of 31 …………. 24-hour shopping/services
• need to examine effects of changing work and sleep habits
• US and British research found these lead to health problems

Main Causes:
A 32 …………
• regulates daily life
• connected to behavioural patterns and cycles of 33 …………
• programmes us to be awake and asleep at certain times

B Sleep Debt
• impossible to get enough sleep during daytime

C 34 ………….
• different working/sleeping times, ‘dislocation’

Effects:
A Physical
• higher incidence of heart and 35 ………… problems
• more minor illnesses, suggesting that immunity of shift workers is affected

B Psychological
• most common: 36 …………….
• 37 ………… affected, e.g. decision-making, planning, which regulate our 38 …………...

C Social
Night shift work can lead to:
• destruction of 39 …………. and other relationships, e.g. 40 …………..
• eventually, for individuals: social isolation
